title: Bananas Foster
imagePrompt: Two scoops of pale vanilla bean ice cream sit in a shallow bowl, their surfaces beginning to soften where the heat meets them. Four banana quarters ring the ice cream, their cut faces glazed in a thick, dark amber caramel that pools and drips down the sides. The sauce is glossy and bubbling at the edges, with flecks of orange zest visible in the rum-spiked caramel.
subtitle: New Orleans flambéed bananas in spice-infused dark caramel. Served over cold vanilla ice cream.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Use a heavy stainless steel or cast-iron skillet — not nonstick. Firm, yellow bananas with green tips hold their shape under heat.
items: Caramel base & spices
¼ cup (½ stick) unsalted butter, cubed
1 cup dark brown sugar, firmly packed
1 tsp Vietnamese or Ceylon cinnamon, ground
¼ tsp whole nutmeg, freshly grated
½ tsp vanilla bean paste (or high-quality bourbon vanilla extract)
1 tsp fresh orange zest
Generous pinch fleur de sel (or flaky sea salt)
Fruit & spirits
4 firm, ripe bananas, peeled and quartered (halved lengthwise, then halved crosswise)
¼ cup (2 oz) banana liqueur (e.g., Giffard or Tempus Fugit)
¼ cup (2 oz) high-proof dark or spiced rum (80–151 proof for a strong flame)
To serve
1 pint premium Madagascar vanilla bean ice cream
ingredients.callout: Banana ripeness: Entirely yellow with green tips and no brown spots. Overripe bananas turn to mush in the hot caramel.

shoppingList.item: Fleur de sel or flaky sea salt

substitutes: Extra rum + ½ tsp banana extract

body.html: A chef-tier take on the Brennan's classic: butter and dark brown sugar caramelized with cinnamon, nutmeg, orange zest, and vanilla, then banana liqueur and rum flambéed tableside-style. Ladled hot over cold vanilla ice cream.

items: Melt butter and build a spice-infused caramel base
Bloom vanilla, orange zest, and salt into the sauce
Caramelize bananas cut-side down, baste, flip
Flambé the rum — baste through the flames
Serve immediately over vanilla ice cream

body.html: Safety first: Turn off the overhead exhaust fan before igniting. Keep a metal lid nearby to smother flames if needed.

slides.title: Build the Spice-Infused Caramel
slides.checkboxLabel: Melt butter, stir in sugar and spices

items: Set a 10–12 inch heavy-bottomed skillet (stainless or cast iron) over medium-low heat.
Melt the butter evenly — do not use nonstick; flambé heat can damage the coating.
Stir in brown sugar, cinnamon, and nutmeg.

body.html: Pan choice matters. You need a pan that can take high heat and an open flame without warping or releasing fumes from a coating.

slides.title: Dissolve and Bloom
slides.checkboxLabel: Stir 3–4 min, add vanilla, zest, salt

items: Stir constantly with a heat-resistant spatula or wooden spoon for 3–4 minutes.
Sugar melts into the butter — the base should be fragrant, bubbling, and glossy.
Stir in vanilla bean paste, orange zest, and fleur de sel to bloom the flavors.

body.html: Don't rush this step. Full sugar dissolution is what gives you the thick, dark amber glaze — not grainy caramel.

slides.title: Caramelize the Bananas
slides.checkboxLabel: Add liqueur, cook bananas 2 min per side

items: Pour in banana liqueur and stir until fully integrated.
Arrange banana quarters in a single layer, cut-side down.
Baste by spooning caramel over the bananas.
Cook undisturbed 2 minutes, then flip and cook 1 more minute until tender but still holding shape.

body.html: Cut-side down first. That's where the caramelization happens. Basting keeps the tops glossy while the bottoms take color.

slides.title: The Flambé
slides.checkboxLabel: Pour rum, ignite vapors — do not stir

items: Pour rum evenly over the bananas — do not stir it in.
Let it heat through ~10 seconds until vapors form on the surface.
Gas stove: Tilt the skillet toward the burner flame to ignite.
Electric: Keep your face back and use a long-reach lighter or match along the skillet edge.

body.html: Turn off the exhaust fan before lighting. A draft can pull flames upward. Keep loose clothing and hair clear of the pan.

slides.title: Baste and Extinguish
slides.checkboxLabel: Shake pan, baste through flames, remove from heat

items: As blue and gold flames erupt, gently shake the skillet by the handle.
Use a long-handled spoon to baste the bananas in the flaming sauce.
Fire dies down naturally after 30–45 seconds as alcohol burns off.
Remove from heat immediately when flames subside.

body.html: The flambé isn't just theater. Burning off the rum concentrates the sauce and adds a warm, boozy depth without raw alcohol bite.

slides.title: Assemble and Serve
slides.checkboxLabel: Ice cream in bowls, bananas + hot sauce on top

items: Scoop two balls of cold vanilla ice cream into shallow serving bowls.
Arrange four warm banana quarters around each scoop.
Generously ladle hot rum-caramel sauce straight from the skillet over the top.
Serve immediately — the hot-cold contrast is the whole point.

body.html: Speed is everything. The sauce keeps cooking in the hot pan. Plate as soon as the flames die — ice cream should still be firm when it hits the table.
